text.title: scrum-ceremonies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for scrum-ceremonies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Ceremony theatre — team runs events but DoR/DoD discipline and action follow-through are missing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ceremonies without underlying discipline produce the appearance of Scrum without the inspect-and-adapt value. Agents that only schedule and notify reinforce theatre by making it effortless.

failure-mode.description: Standup as management reporting — standup updates go to a manager dashboard, not the team channel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reporting-oriented standups shift power from the self-organizing team to the manager, eroding psychological safety and turning the event into a performance rather than a coordination mechanism.

failure-mode.description: Retros without action follow-through — same actions appear sprint after sprint with no resolution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Repeated unresolved actions signal that the team does not believe retro actions will change anything. Trust in the retro collapses; teams stop contributing honestly.

failure-mode.description: PO convergence in planning poker — PO names a number first and team votes to match it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Authority-anchored poker produces uniform votes from the first round. No disagreement means no discussion, which means the only person whose estimate was real was the PO — poker served no purpose.

failure-mode.description: Async blocker rot — a blocker is posted in standup, no one responds, it ages past 24 hours in a thread.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unresolved async blockers compound daily. A 24-hour blocker in a 10-day sprint consumes 10% of capacity silently. Hard limit: blocker older than 1 working day must trigger a sync or escalation.
